title: asparagus officinalis subsp. litoralis
intro: Asparagus officinalis subsp. litoralis, also known as sea asparagus or beach asparagus, is a subspecies of the asparagus officinalis plant that is native to coastal areas and thrives in salt-tolerant environments. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of asparagus officinalis subsp. litoralis include supporting kidney function, reducing blood pressure, and providing antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, sea asparagus can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or added to salads and other dishes for its unique flavor and potential health benefits.
